verdicts.interest_take: The sharpest idea in the hour: tolerance-as-shrug isn't the same as showing up for someone.
verdicts.writing_notes: Idea is the real payoff and cleanly distinct from moment; warm, honest about the team fumbling; dignified.

verdicts.interest_take: Humane distinction most shows blur — hurt-from-hiding vs. bigotry — recasting the red card as loyalty.
verdicts.writing_notes: Precise, moving idea; slur handled by reference only, never reproduced; right category.

verdicts.interest_take: The title states the theme before a word is spoken — a real 'I never caught that.'
verdicts.writing_notes: Lineage clear and concise; idea is the payoff, not the moment; dignified framing.

verdicts.interest_take: The musical's proudest song bookends the hour — declaring identity without apology.
verdicts.writing_notes: Pairs cleanly with the title card without duplicating it (song-as-bookend vs title-as-thesis); warm.

verdicts.interest_take: Genuine craft catch: the words are never heard — privacy given even as the team is given.
verdicts.writing_notes: Restraint reading is fresh and distinct from the belonging cards; honest that it's a reading, not creator intent.

verdicts.interest_take: The spine of the episode: wanting two lives to be one, belonging over spectacle.
verdicts.writing_notes: Lower surprise (it's the obvious throughline) but earns its place as the anchor; clean and warm.

verdicts.interest_take: Off-screen context — the 32-year silence between Fashanu and Daniels — that explains why the arc lands.
verdicts.writing_notes: Sober and dignified; Fashanu's death handled with care; the only history card, good variety.

verdicts.interest_take: The gruffest man wins the room by teaching empathy — Roy adopting the Lasso method.
verdicts.writing_notes: Strong B-plot beat, distinct from Colin's arc; good category; absorbs the Rebecca setup beat below.

verdicts.interest_take: Makes the coming-out a relay, not a solo act — visibility handed person to person.
verdicts.writing_notes: Warm and dignified; weakest of the keepers (relevance leans on S3E6, partially-confirmed) but adds a tender note; flag the earlier-episode lean.

verdicts.interest_take: Recap-ish payoff (he plays great after coming out); overlaps the belonging/visibility theme card.
verdicts.writing_notes: Idea duplicates 'hiding is labor, being seen frees you' already carried by the spine card; weakest of the Colin cluster.

verdicts.interest_take: Connective setup scene for the presser; lower interest, no standalone idea the presser card lacks.
verdicts.writing_notes: Setup/payoff overlap with the Roy presser card; tighten by keeping only the payoff.

verdicts.interest_take: Lightest payoff in the set (insight 2); a sitcom-trivia wink that jars against a dignified coming-out hour.
verdicts.writing_notes: Tonally off for this episode's set; trim to keep the collection tight and on-theme.

episode_notes: Strong episode, but 12 was bloated. Cut 3 for a tight 9. The Colin/visibility arc had three overlapping cards ('two lives', 'plays free', 'never said aloud'); kept the spine (belonging) plus the genuine craft catch (no spoken line), cut 'plays free' as a recap-ish duplicate of the same thesis. Roy's beat was split into setup ('Rebecca pushes Roy') and payoff ('press conference'); kept the payoff, cut the connective setup. Cut the Office 'that's what she said' gag as the lightest, most tonally off card in a dignified coming-out hour. Remaining set has good variety: ethics (allyship, empathy), relationships (betrayal-not-bigotry, mentorship), aesthetics (title, closing song, the unspoken line), the belonging spine, and one sober real-world history card. The slur is referenced only, never reproduced, throughout. Ordering puts the two sharpest ideas first (allyship-past-tolerance, hurt-not-hate), then the elegant title/song pair, then craft and theme, with the lower-relevance Trent mentor card last. Trent card flagged 'forward' since its foundation is S3E6 ('Sunflowers').
